commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.

- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)

- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

However, the layout has a critical flaw: filtered elements break grid positioning because they're rendered in document order rather than maintaining their original grid positions—cells will collapse/reflow instead of staying in their periodic table positions. Additionally, the legend code is incomplete (truncated), and hover/focus states on interactive elements lack smooth visual feedback.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

evaluation.critique: The component has good data handling and hierarchy, but the responsive grid system is incomplete (CardGrid imported but never used, no explicit responsive columns defined). Design tokens are partially applied with unnecessary fallback hex values undermining their purpose, and the layout lacks mobile-first clarity with the promised '3 per row desktop, 1 mobile' grid unimplemented.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.sourceCode:
import React from 'react';
import { Box, Card, Divider, Heading, Stack, Text } from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface Props {
  /** City name */
  city: string;
  /** Current temperature */
  temperature: number;
  /** Weather condition (Sunny, Cloudy, Rainy, etc.) */
  condition: string;
  /** Humidity percentage */
  humidity: number;
  /** Wind speed */
  windSpeed: number;
  /** Temperature unit (C or F) */
  unit?: string;
  /** 5-day forecast array */
  forecast: Array<{ day?: string; icon?: string; high?: number; low?: number }>;
}

function getWeatherSymbol(conditionText?: string): string {
  const normalized = (conditionText || '').toLowerCase();

  if (normalized.includes('thunder') || normalized.includes('storm')) return '⛈️';
  if (normalized.includes('snow') || normalized.includes('sleet') || normalized.includes('ice')) return '❄️';
  if (normalized.includes('rain') || normalized.includes('drizzle') || normalized.includes('shower')) return '🌧️';
  if (normalized.includes('cloud') || normalized.includes('overcast')) return '☁️';
  if (normalized.includes('fog') || normalized.includes('mist') || normalized.includes('haze')) return '🌫️';
  if (normalized.includes('wind')) return '💨';
  if (normalized.includes('sun') || normalized.includes('clear')) return '☀️';

  return '•';
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({
  city = 'Sample City',
  temperature = 22,
  condition = 'Sunny',
  humidity = 58,
  windSpeed = 12,
  unit = 'C',
  forecast = [
    { day: 'Mon', icon: '☀️', high: 24, low: 16 },
    { day: 'Tue', icon: '⛅', high: 22, low: 15 },
    { day: 'Wed', icon: '🌧️', high: 19, low: 13 },
    { day: 'Thu', icon: '☁️', high: 21, low: 14 },
    { day: 'Fri', icon: '☀️', high: 25, low: 17 },
  ],
}: Props) {
  const resolvedUnit = unit || 'C';
  const currentIcon = getWeatherSymbol(condition);
  const visibleForecast = forecast.slice(0, 5);

  return (
    <Box aria-label={`Weather for ${city}`}>
      <Card
        shadow="md"
        radius="xl"
        padding="var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)"
        border
      >
        <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)">
          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)">
            <Stack direction="horizontal" justify="between" align="start" wrap>
              <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
                <Text
                  variant="overline"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                >
                  {city}
                </Text>
                <Heading
                  level={1}
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                >
                  {temperature}°{resolvedUnit}
                </Heading>
                <Text
                  variant="bodyLarge"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                >
                  {condition}
                </Text>
              </Stack>

              <Box
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
                background="var(--ggui-color-container, #ede9fe)"
                borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-xl, 16px)"
              >
                <Stack align="center" gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
                  <Text
                    as="span"
                    size="4xl"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onContainer, #4c1d95)"
                  >
                    {currentIcon}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onContainer, #4c1d95)"
                    align="center"
                  >
                    {condition}
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Box>
            </Stack>

            <Stack direction="horizontal" gap={12} wrap>
              <Box
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
                background="var(--ggui-color-surface, #fafafa)"
                borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-lg, 12px)"
              >
                <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                  >
                    Humidity
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="label"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                  >
                    {humidity}%
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Box>

              <Box
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
                background="var(--ggui-color-surface, #fafafa)"
                borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-lg, 12px)"
              >
                <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                  >
                    Wind speed
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="label"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                  >
                    {windSpeed}
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Box>
            </Stack>
          </Stack>

          <Divider color="var(--ggui-color-outline, #d4d4d8)" margin={0} />

          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)">
            <Text
              variant="overline"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            >
              Forecast
            </Text>

            <Box aria-label="5-day forecast">
              <Stack
                direction="horizontal"
                justify="between"
                align="stretch"
                gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
                wrap={false}
              >
                {visibleForecast.map((item, index) => {
                  const dayLabel = item.day || '—';
                  const iconLabel = item.icon || getWeatherSymbol(condition);
                  const highLabel = `${item.high ?? '--'}°`;
                  const lowLabel = `${item.low ?? '--'}°`;

                  return (
                    <Box
                      key={`${item.day || 'forecast'}-${index}`}
                      padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
                      background="var(--ggui-color-surface, #fafafa)"
                      borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-lg, 12px)"
                      aria-label={`${dayLabel}: high ${highLabel}, low ${lowLabel}, ${iconLabel}`}
                    >
                      <Stack align="center" gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
                        <Text
                          variant="caption"
                          color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                          align="center"
                        >
                          {dayLabel}
                        </Text>
                        <Text
                          as="span"
                          size="xl"
                          color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                        >
                          {iconLabel}
                        </Text>
                        <Stack gap={0} align="center">
                          <Text
                            variant="caption"
                            weight="semibold"
                            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                            align="center"
                          >
                            H: {highLabel}
                          </Text>
                          <Text
                            variant="caption"
                            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                            align="center"
                          >
                            L: {lowLabel}
                          </Text>
                        </Stack>
                      </Stack>
                    </Box>
                  );
                })}
              </Stack>
            </Box>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Box>
  );
}

export { type Props };

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates strong design token usage and proper data handling from props, but has layout issues: the forecast strip uses `wrap={false}` which causes horizontal overflow on small screens, and spacing consistency uses both token variables and hardcoded pixel fallbacks (e.g., `gap={12}`). Missing hover/focus states on interactive forecast items and no loading/error states despite being a data-driven weather component.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:

- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.
